Beeble launches AI-powered Background Remover rotoscoping tool
By
Jake Bickerton
2026-04-07T16:23:00+01:00
No comments
It automatically generates high-quality foreground masks from video, images and sequences, with support for batch processing
Beeble has launched Background Remover, an AI-powered rotoscoping tool it says delivers “clean, detailed alpha mattes in seconds”.
It enables creators to isolate subjects from complex footage with “speed, precision, and full creative control,” says Beeble.
Background Remover uses advanced AI models to automatically generate high-quality foreground masks from video, images, or image sequences.
Beeble says it can handle complex motion, occlusion, and fine detail such as hair and motion blur.
Users can process up to 20 video or image files at once, or work with image sequences of up to 2,000 frames.
A preview frame is generated instantly, enabling results to be assessed before committing to full processing.
For more complex shots, an interactive masking interface enables precise subject selection, giving users control over exactly what remains in frame.
Background Remover supports a range of export formats designed for post-production environments. These include 10-bit RGBA video files with embedded alpha channels, RGBA PNG sequences for frame-by-frame compositing, and alpha-only outputs in both video and image sequence formats.
Background Remover is available now on the Beeble Web App.
